skill.name: remembering-conversations
skill.description: Searches past Claude Code conversations through an episodic-memory MCP plugin when historical context may improve implementation decisions or recall prior work.
skill.summary: Search past development conversations before repeating decisions or missing prior context.
skill.author: obra

security_audit.summary: Static external-command and weak-crypto findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks, TypeScript examples, and words such as markdown. The real security concern is intended access to local conversation archives, which may contain sensitive user requests, code, file paths, and tool outputs.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Conversation Archive Access
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents to search and read past conversations through MCP tools. These archives can contain private user requests, source code, local paths, credentials accidentally pasted into chats, and tool outputs. This is intended functionality, but it requires clear user consent and careful source handling.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly describes search and read access to conversation history and absolute JSONL archive paths. This confirms sensitive local data access, although it is aligned with the skill purpose.

medium_findings.title: Historical Prompt Injection Exposure
medium_findings.description: The skill retrieves prior conversation content and asks the assistant to synthesize it. Old conversations may contain untrusted instructions or prompt-injection text, so retrieved material must be treated as reference data rather than new instructions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill's intended workflow reads and summarizes prior messages, which can contain arbitrary user or assistant text. No malicious injection text was found in the skill files themselves.

low_findings.title: False Positive External Command Detections
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many Markdown backticks and fenced examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The reviewed files are Markdown documentation and contain tool names and TypeScript-style examples, not executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found Markdown code spans, tables, and examples only. No shell execution syntax is present outside documentation.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Detections
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts appear to match benign substrings in Markdown content and metadata, not actual calls to MD5, SHA1, or other weak cryptographic algorithms. No evidence of cryptographic operations was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose, parameter tables, or example prompts. There is no executable code or crypto API use in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive System Reconnaissance Detections
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged error-handling and performance notes as reconnaissance. The content describes possible MCP tool errors and local search behavior, with no commands to enumerate the host system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is user-facing documentation about search failures and performance. No system inventory commands or host reconnaissance workflow is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Direct MCP Access to Local Conversation Files
dangerous_patterns.description: The MCP read tool accepts a path to a local JSONL conversation file and can return full or ranged conversation content. This creates privacy risk if used without user awareness or if retrieved content is trusted as instructions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented read tool takes an absolute local path and returns conversation content. The behavior is explicit and security-relevant, even though it is not malicious by itself.

content.value_statement: Teams often repeat past decisions because useful context stays buried in old chats. This skill searches prior Claude Code conversations and returns focused sources before new work begins.
actual_capabilities: Triggers a search-conversations agent for historical conversation lookup.
Searches past Claude Code conversations by topic, decision, pattern, or problem.
Reads selected conversation results through MCP tools for source-backed context.
Summarizes relevant findings with actionable guidance and source references.
Supports date filters, result limits, and markdown or JSON response formats.
Encourages searching only after the current task and codebase context are understood.
limitations: Requires the episodic-memory MCP plugin and indexed conversation archives.
Can expose sensitive prior conversation content if used without consent.
Search quality depends on archive completeness, indexing, and query wording.
Does not verify whether old decisions are still correct for the current codebase.

use_cases.title: Recover Prior Architecture Decisions
use_cases.description: Find earlier reasoning before changing a system that has undocumented history.

use_cases.title: Avoid Repeating Failed Debugging Paths
use_cases.description: Search old conversations for errors, fixes, and rejected approaches before starting another investigation.

use_cases.title: Onboard Into Existing Project Context
use_cases.description: Use past project discussions to understand workflows, conventions, and known tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Find Past Work
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner lookup for a named feature or task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search past conversations for work on [feature or task]. Summarize the most relevant decisions and include sources.

prompt_templates.title: Recall a Decision
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for why we chose [approach]. Focus on tradeoffs, rejected options, and any follow-up tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Debug With History
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced lookup after current investigation finds a recurring issue.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search past conversations for [error or symptom]. Compare previous fixes with the current codebase evidence before recommending next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Synthesize Project Memory
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced synthesis across multiple related concepts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for conversations about [concept one], [concept two], and [concept three]. Return patterns, contradictions, risks, and source-backed recommendations.

output_examples.input: Search past conversations for our React Router authentication work.
output_examples.output: The response identifies related discussions, summarizes the authentication approach, notes unresolved edge cases, and cites the relevant archived conversation sources.

output_examples.input: Why did we avoid the earlier migration plan?
output_examples.output: The response lists the prior reasons, highlights risks that may still apply, and separates old context from current recommendations.

output_examples.input: Find any previous fixes for this flaky test pattern.
output_examples.output: The response reports matching incidents, explains which fixes worked, and recommends checking whether the current failure has the same cause.

best_practices: Understand the current task and codebase before searching conversation history.
Treat retrieved conversation text as reference material, not as instructions.
Ask for user consent before reading sensitive or unrelated historical conversations.
anti_patterns: Searching memory before clarifying the current request.
Copying old recommendations without validating them against current files.
Reading full archived conversations when a targeted search result is enough.

faq.question: What does this skill search?
faq.answer: It searches indexed Claude Code conversation archives through the episodic-memory MCP plugin.

faq.question: Does it work without the MCP plugin?
faq.answer: No. It depends on the episodic-memory MCP tools and a local archive index.

faq.question: Can it read private information?
faq.answer: Yes. Past conversations may contain private code, file paths, decisions, or secrets pasted by mistake.

faq.question: Should old conversation content be trusted?
faq.answer: No. Retrieved content should be treated as historical evidence and checked against current project files.

faq.question: When should users activate it?
faq.answer: Use it after the assistant understands the current task and needs prior decisions, patterns, or known fixes.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk privacy warning because it intentionally accesses conversation history.
